# Workload Analysis Report
*Generated by `wokload_analyser.py` — Berkeley Motif classification (extended)*

## Summary

| # | Motif | Apps | % of Total |
|--:|-------|-----:|-----------:|

| 1 | Stencil / PDE | 64 | 34.0% |
| 2 | Dense Linear Algebra (DLA) | 59 | 31.4% |
| 3 | Element-wise / Reduction | 24 | 12.8% |
| 4 | General / Infrastructure | 13 | 6.9% |
| 5 | Sparse / Graph | 9 | 4.8% |
| 6 | Mesh / Geometry | 9 | 4.8% |
| 7 | MapReduce / Data Parallel | 7 | 3.7% |
| 8 | N-Body / Particle | 3 | 1.6% |

---

## Kernel Motif Reference

| Kernel Motif | Underlying Operations | Optimisation Focus | RISC-V Notes |
|---|---|---|---|

| **Dense Linear Algebra (DLA)** | GEMM, GEMV, LU, Cholesky, QR, Eigensolvers | Register tiling, RVV vectorisation, L1/L2 cache blocking, BLAS-3 kernel reuse | Primary RVV target; VLEN-512 achieves ~90 % peak on GEMM. Use RISC-V V intrinsics for inner loop. |
| **Stencil / PDE** | N-point stencil averaging, halo exchange, 3-D/4-D grid iteration, AMR | Cache blocking, prefetching, memory-bandwidth bound, data alignment, MPI halo | Bandwidth-bound; use RVV gather for irregular stencils. Benefit from large VLEN for unit-stride sweeps. |
| **Spectral / FFT** | FFT, butterfly networks, convolution, spectral differentiation | Cache reuse, bit-reversal, twiddle-factor caching, RVV butterfly | Implement Cooley-Tukey with RVV; target radix-8 for VLEN≥512. Mixed-radix for non-power-of-2. |
| **Sparse / Graph** | SpMV, SpGEMM, BFS/DFS, gather-scatter, CSR/CSC format | Irregular memory access, gather/scatter latency, CSR row-balance, load balancing | RVV indexed gather for SpMV; exploit non-temporal stores for write-once rows. Software prefetch for CSR indices. |
| **N-Body / Particle** | All-pairs force, tree traversal, neighbour lists, Ewald summation, particle-in-cell | Spatial locality, neighbour-list caching, tree-code vectorisation, SIMD force kernel | Inner force loop vectorises cleanly with RVV FP32/FP64. Use masked ops for pair-exclusion lists. |
| **MapReduce / Data Parallel** | Map, Shuffle, Reduce, Sort, Scan, ETL | Memory bandwidth, cache-oblivious layout, vectorised reduction | RVV reductions (vredsum, vredmax) directly accelerate map-reduce inner loops. |
| **Element-wise / Reduction** | Add, Mul, ReLU, Softmax, Norm, Sum, Dot product, Transcendentals | Kernel fusion, loop unrolling, RVV FP reductions, mixed precision | Fuse activation + normalisation into single RVV pass. Use vfmacc for fused multiply-accumulate. |
| **Mesh / Geometry** | Mesh generation, ray-tracing, surface normals, BVH traversal, CAD kernel | Memory layout for irregular access, BVH stack, SIMD triangle intersection | BVH traversal benefits from RVV masked compare; triangle batch intersection with fp32 RVV. |
| **General / Infrastructure** | Workload management, profiling, build, embedded control, protocol stacks | Latency, I/O throughput, OS scheduling, lightweight runtime | Bare-metal RISC-V runtime; CLIC interrupt controller for real-time embedded. Use Zicsr for perf counters. |

---

## Per-Motif App Listing

### Stencil / PDE — 64 apps (34.0%)

- **Operations:** N-point stencil averaging, halo exchange, 3-D/4-D grid iteration, AMR
- **Optimisation focus:** Cache blocking, prefetching, memory-bandwidth bound, data alignment, MPI halo
- **RISC-V notes:** Bandwidth-bound; use RVV gather for irregular stencils. Benefit from large VLEN for unit-stride sweeps.

### Dense Linear Algebra (DLA) — 59 apps (31.4%)

- **Operations:** GEMM, GEMV, LU, Cholesky, QR, Eigensolvers
- **Optimisation focus:** Register tiling, RVV vectorisation, L1/L2 cache blocking, BLAS-3 kernel reuse
- **RISC-V notes:** Primary RVV target; VLEN-512 achieves ~90 % peak on GEMM. Use RISC-V V intrinsics for inner loop.

### Element-wise / Reduction — 24 apps (12.8%)

- **Operations:** Add, Mul, ReLU, Softmax, Norm, Sum, Dot product, Transcendentals
- **Optimisation focus:** Kernel fusion, loop unrolling, RVV FP reductions, mixed precision
- **RISC-V notes:** Fuse activation + normalisation into single RVV pass. Use vfmacc for fused multiply-accumulate.

### General / Infrastructure — 13 apps (6.9%)

- **Operations:** Workload management, profiling, build, embedded control, protocol stacks
- **Optimisation focus:** Latency, I/O throughput, OS scheduling, lightweight runtime
- **RISC-V notes:** Bare-metal RISC-V runtime; CLIC interrupt controller for real-time embedded. Use Zicsr for perf counters.

### Sparse / Graph — 9 apps (4.8%)

- **Operations:** SpMV, SpGEMM, BFS/DFS, gather-scatter, CSR/CSC format
- **Optimisation focus:** Irregular memory access, gather/scatter latency, CSR row-balance, load balancing
- **RISC-V notes:** RVV indexed gather for SpMV; exploit non-temporal stores for write-once rows. Software prefetch for CSR indices.

### Mesh / Geometry — 9 apps (4.8%)

- **Operations:** Mesh generation, ray-tracing, surface normals, BVH traversal, CAD kernel
- **Optimisation focus:** Memory layout for irregular access, BVH stack, SIMD triangle intersection
- **RISC-V notes:** BVH traversal benefits from RVV masked compare; triangle batch intersection with fp32 RVV.

### N-Body / Particle — 3 apps (1.6%)

- **Operations:** All-pairs force, tree traversal, neighbour lists, Ewald summation, particle-in-cell
- **Optimisation focus:** Spatial locality, neighbour-list caching, tree-code vectorisation, SIMD force kernel
- **RISC-V notes:** Inner force loop vectorises cleanly with RVV FP32/FP64. Use masked ops for pair-exclusion lists.
